evidence: In 2024, we established a company-wide cross-functional team to assess the risks and opportunities from conventional and generative AI. We continued these assessments in 2025 and expect to continue these efforts going forward.
we established a company-wide cross-functional team to assess the risks and opportunities from conventional and generative AI
We continued these assessments in 2025 and expect to continue these efforts going forward
AI may also be leveraged by threat actors to enhance the volume and sophistication of their attacks.

Our vendors and suppliers are also increasingly using and offering platforms powered by AI... we have limited control over ensuring that third parties themselves are consistently enforcing strong controls
The growing sophistication and implementation of advanced AI technologies also increases the risks we face related to data privacy and security.

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI spend, headcount, or named vendor/infrastructure partnerships disclosed; only a cross-functional assessment team is mentioned, yielding a score of 2.
ai_narrative_concreteness: AI discussion is limited to risk assessment activities and aspirational framing; no named deployed use cases or production systems described.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and discusses AI risks with company-specific mechanisms (third-party vendor AI exposure, threat-actor use of AI, data privacy amplification), supporting a score of 4; however, no quantified risk treatment or named governance body specific to AI risk is disclosed.
